results.title: 2025 Coolest Tool Award
results.description: The sixth edition of the Coolest Tool Award happened at Wikimania in Nairobi, Kenya in August 2025. See https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Coolest_Tool_Award/2025 for more details.

tools.name: toolforge-paulina
tools.title: Paulina - Data for the Public Domain
tools.description: A global, multilingual search interface for works and authors in the public domain using Wikidata and Python.

tools.name: plwiki-msz2001-abusefilter-analyzer
tools.title: AbuseFilter analyzer
tools.description: Parses AbuseFilter conditions and evaluates them.

tools.name: toolforge-sdzerobot
tools.title: SDZeroBot
tools.description: Various bot tasks on English Wikipedia

results.title: 2024 Coolest Tool Award
results.description: The fifth edition of the Coolest Tool Award happened at Wikimania in Katowice, Poland in August 2024. See https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Coolest_Tool_Award/2024 for more details.

tools.name: toolforge-view-it
tools.title: View it!
tools.description:
View it! is a tool to show Wikipedia editors and readers relevant Wikimedia Commons media depicting—or otherwise related to—the article they are reading. 

https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/View_it!_Tool

tools.name: enwiki-diegodlh-web2cit
tools.title: Web2Cit
tools.description: Collaboratively improved automatic citation results

tools.name: isa
tools.title: ISA Tool : Easily Add Information to Images on Wikimedia Commons
tools.description:
ISA is a multilingual, mobile-first micro-contributions tool, that makes it easy for people to add structured data to images on Wikimedia Commons.
Logged-in Wikimedians can create dedicated campaigns with the goal of improving one or several specific categories of images on Wikimedia Commons, within a certain time frame. Any logged-in wikimedian can then participate to the campaign adding structured metadata. 
The tool is designed to be a host of small competitions. In particular, the tool provides stats that allow to identify the best contributors to the said campaign real time ! Participation info is provided in a nice visual format that can be directly used to include in coordinator report or used to communicate the outcome of the competition on social media, turning the campaign into a communication tool. Data about each campaign participation is also available for download into cvs format for any further statistical uses.

tools.name: enwiki-קיפודנחש-cat-a-lot
tools.title: Cat-a-lot
tools.description: {{anchor|Cat-a-lot}}Extremely powerful category editing tool from Commons. Works with regular articles, as well as files. This is a wrapper that sets the setting variable to enable non-file categorization, and loads the latest Cat-a-lot from Commons.

tools.name: integraality
tools.title: inteGraality
tools.description: Generate dashboards of property coverage for a given part of Wikidata.

tools.name: wikidata-walkabout
tools.title: Wikidata Walkabout
tools.description: A drill-down navigator for Wikidata.

tools.name: toolforge-campwiz
tools.title: a tool designed to streamline and manage Wikipedia edit-a-thons
tools.description: CampWiz is a tool designed to streamline and manage Wikipedia edit-a-thons, particularly those focused on cultural diversity and gender inclusivity, like the Feminism and Folklore Editathon, by offering features for event management, submission tracking, and evaluation

results.title: 2022 Coolest Tool Award
results.description: The fourth edition of the Coolest Tool Award happened virtually on Friday, December 16th, 2022. See https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Coolest_Tool_Award/2022 for more details.

tools.name: citationhunt
tools.title: Citation Hunt
tools.description: Citation Hunt is a tool for browsing snippets of Wikipedia articles that lack citations. It is available in several languages and allows browsing snippets either randomly or by their article's categories in a quick and fun way.

tools.name: metawiki-musikanimal-moremenu
tools.title: MoreMenu
tools.description: Extends the "More" menu by adding two more drop-down menus to your menubar.

tools.name: mm_petscan
tools.title: PetScan
tools.description: PetScan can generate lists of Wikipedia (and related projects) pages or Wikidata items that match certain criteria, such as all pages in a certain category, or all items with a certain property. PetScan can also combine some temporary lists (here called 'sources') in various ways, to create a new one.

tools.name: patchdemo
tools.title: Patch demo
tools.description: With Patch demo, you too can spin up a MediaWiki instance running a particular patch from Wikimedia Gerrit.

tools.name: toolforge-bullseye
tools.title: Bullseye: Extended IP info
tools.description: An at-a-glance view of information about an IP address, consolidated from multiple sources

tools.name: toolforge-scholia
tools.title: Wikidata scholarly profile
tools.description: Webservice running Python Flask with scholarly profiles generated via the Wikidata Query Service.

tools.name: viwiki-plantaest-twinklemobile
tools.title: TwinkleMobile
tools.description: Enables Twinkle on mobile view (Minerva skin).

tools.name: openrefine
tools.title: OpenRefine
tools.description: A free data wrangling tool to work with messy data, to clean and improve it, and to connect it with knowledge bases. You can use OpenRefine to (batch) edit Wikidata, to edit and add structured data to files on Wikimedia Commons, and to upload files to Wikimedia Commons.

results.title: 2021 Coolest Tool Award
results.description: The third edition of the Coolest Tool Award happened virtually on Friday 14 January 2022. Learn more at https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Coolest_Tool_Award/2021

tools.name: mm_wikishootme
tools.title: WikiShootMe
tools.description: WikiShootMe V3 (WSM) is a tool to show Wikidata items, Wikipedia articles, and Commons images with coordinates, all on the same map.

tools.name: wikimedia-quarry
tools.title: Quarry
tools.description: Quarry is a public querying interface for Wiki Replicas, a set of live replica SQL databases of public Wikimedia Wikis. Quarry is designed to make running queries against Wiki Replicas easy. Quarry also provides a means for researchers to share and review each other's queries. Users of Quarry are required to agree to Cloud Services Terms of use.

tools.name: wmcloud-humaniki
tools.title: Humaniki
tools.description: A dashboard tool that helps explore gender diversity in Wikimedia projects. Humaniki provides statistics about the gender gap in the content of all Wikimedia projects based on data available on Wikidata. The data is available under the creative commons license and is free for anyone to use!

tools.name: hay-depictor
tools.title: Depictor
tools.description: Add structured data statements to Wikimedia Commons using a game-like interface

tools.name: metawiki-jon-harald-søby-diffedit
tools.title: diffedit
tools.description: Enables editing directly from viewing a diff.

tools.name: cd
tools.title: Convenient Discussions
tools.description: Convenient Discussions (CD) is a JavaScript tool providing a shell over the existing MediaWiki discussion system that enhances user experience with talk pages in multiple ways.

tools.name: wudele
tools.title: Wudele
tools.description: Schedule an event or make a poll

tools.name: wikimedia-paws
tools.title: PAWS
tools.description:
PAWS: A Web Shell (PAWS) is a Jupyter notebook deployment hosted by Wikimedia.

A Jupyter notebook is a popular Open Source tool that allows to create and share documents that contain live code. You can use Jupyter notebooks to run scripts that help you perform essential technical tasks on wikis, use data to create visualizations, graphs, and more, and to write techinical documentation and tutorials that help others work on Wikimedia projects.

Jupyter notebooks are used both by experienced programmers and technically curious newcomers. There's no need to download software or set up a development environment. All of your work is done in your browser.

Our deployment is using JupyterHub, which can serve live Jupyter notebooks to multiple users.

tools.name: toolforge-copyvios
tools.title: Earwig's Copyvio Detector
tools.description: A copyright violation detector that can search the web for article content and compare pages with specific URLs.

results.title: 2020 Coolest Tool Award
results.description: The second edition of the Coolest Tool Award happened virtually on 11 December 2020, 17:00 UTC, live streamed on Youtube in the MediaWiki channel. Learn more at https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Coolest_Tool_Award/2020

tools.name: autowikibrowser
tools.title: AutoWikiBrowser
tools.description: AutoWikiBrowser (AWB) is a semi-automated MediaWiki editor designed to make tedious or repetitive editing tasks quicker and easier. It is a .NET desktop application which includes a browser that follows a user-generated list of pages to modify, presenting changes to implement within each of those pages, then progressing to the next page in the list once the changes are confirmed or skipped by the user. When set to do so, it suggests some changes (typically formatting) that are generally meant to be incidental to the main change.

tools.name: toolforge-sdzerobot
tools.title: SDZeroBot
tools.description: Various bot tasks on English Wikipedia

tools.name: hatnote-listen
tools.title: Listen to Wikipedia
tools.description: Listen to the sound of Wikipedia's recent changes feed. Bells indicate additions and string plucks indicate subtractions. Pitch changes according to the size of the edit; the larger the edit, the deeper the note. Green circles show edits from unregistered contributors, and purple circles mark edits performed by automated bots. You may see announcements for new users as they join the site, punctuated by a string swell. You can welcome him or her by clicking the blue banner and adding a note on their talk page.

tools.name: pywikibot
tools.title: Pywikibot
tools.description: Python library and collection of scripts that automate work on MediaWiki sites

tools.name: wmfr-lingua-libre
tools.title: Lingua Libre
tools.description: Lingua Libre is a tool that allows to record a large number of words in a few hours (up to 1,000 words/hour with a clean word list and an experienced user). It automatizes the classic procedure for recording and adding audio-visual pronunciation files onto Wikimedia projects. Once the recording is done, the platform automatically uploads clean, well cut, well named and apps-friendly audio files, directly to Wikimedia Commons.

tools.name: toolforge-croptool
tools.title: CropTool
tools.description: CropTool is a MediaWiki tool for cropping images at Wikimedia Commons and other Wikimedia sites. It supports JPEG, PNG, TIFF and (animated) GIF files, and can also extract single pages from DJVU and PDF files as JPEG for cropping. CropTool is inspired by User:Cropbot, but adds some new features, such as OAuth for authorization and automatic border detection.

tools.name: x-tools-xtools
tools.title: XTools
tools.description: A suite of tools to analyze page, user and project data of MediaWiki websites.

results.title: 2019 Coolest Tool Award
results.description: The first ever Coolest Tool Award ceremony took place during Wikimania 2019, as part of the Wikimania program. We have recognized some of the greatest tools in a variety of categories: Experience, Tiny, Impact, Reusable, Editor, Developer, Mobile, Newcomer, Outreach, Eggbeater (= tools that have been around for a long time). Learn more at https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Coolest_Tool_Award/2019

tools.name: toolforge-locator-tool
tools.title: locator-tool
tools.description:
Tool to add {{Location}} or {{Object location}} to images on Wikimedia Commons

Fetch images on Wikimedia Commons via category or user contributions, shows markers for {{Location}} or {{Object location}} on map, allows to change {{Location}} or {{Object location}} and save the description page.

tools.name: commons-gadget-hotcat
tools.title: HotCat
tools.description: Gadget that augments the category bar with quick links to remove, change, or add categories.

tools.name: iabot
tools.title: IABot Management Interface
tools.description: InternetArchiveBot is an advanced bot that identifies and replaces broken external links. Use this interface to run the bot on one or more pages, or to configure its operation on a wiki.

tools.name: pageviews
tools.title: Pageviews Analysis
tools.description: Analysis of pageviews across multiple pages

tools.name: quickstatements
tools.title: QuickStatements
tools.description: Tool to edit Wikidata items, based on a simple set of text commands. The tool can add and remove statements, labels, descriptions and aliases; as well as add statements with optional qualifiers and sources.

tools.name: wmcloud-codesearch
tools.title: MediaWiki Codesearch
tools.description: MediaWiki Codesearch is a fully free software tool that lets you easily search through all of MediaWiki core, extensions, and skins that are hosted on Gerrit. You can limit your search to specific repositories, or types of repositories too. Regular expressions are supported in both the search string, and when filtering by path.

tools.name: android-commons-app
tools.title: Commons Mobile App
tools.description: The Commons Mobile App is a community-maintained app that makes it easy to upload your pictures to Wikimedia Commons. The app can be installed on any Android device and uses your Commons user account to upload photos.

tools.name: programs-and-events-dashboard
tools.title: Programs and Events Dashboard
tools.description: A comprehensive app to organize and track Edit-a-thons, Education Program classes, and other programs. Features include training modules, automatically updated editing stats, article quality visualizations, and more.

tools.name: enwiki-gadget-twinkle
tools.title: Twinkle
tools.description: Twinkle is a popular JavaScript Wikipedia gadget that allows autoconfirmed users to have many extra options to assist them in common Wikipedia maintenance tasks and to help them deal with acts of vandalism or unconstructive edits. It provides users with three types of rollback functions and includes a full library of speedy deletion functions, user warnings and welcomes, maintenance tags, semi-automatic reporting of vandals, and more. In addition, it gives administrators more tools to help them carry out their duties.

results.title: Wikidata Power Tools
results.description: For when you feel like making several million edits to Wikidata

tools.name: mm_wikidata_todo
tools.title: Wikidata Todo
tools.description: Shows you little things you can do on Wikidata.

tools.name: mm_find_duplicate_items
tools.title: Find duplicate items
tools.description: Find duplicate items on Wikidata, via labels/aliases.

tools.name: mm_wikidata_game
tools.title: The Wikidata Game
tools.description: A set of games to quickly add statements to Wikidata.

tools.name: toolforge-shexstatements
tools.title: ShExStatements
tools.description: ShExStatements allows the users to generate entity schemas and shape expressions from simple CSV statements.

tools.name: toolforge-commons-mass-description
tools.title: Commons Mass Description
tools.description: Add description to Commons images en masse

tools.name: quickstatements
tools.title: QuickStatements
tools.description: Tool to edit Wikidata items, based on a simple set of text commands. The tool can add and remove statements, labels, descriptions and aliases; as well as add statements with optional qualifiers and sources.

tools.name: toolforge-harvesting-data-refinery
tools.title: Harvesting Data Refinery
tools.description: Tool allowing you to clean data you added to Wikidata. It will show you the data you added and with one click you'll revert your own edit.

tools.name: toolforge-ranker
tools.title: Ranker
tools.description: tool to edit the rank of multiple Wikidata statements at once

tools.name: mm_mixnmatch
tools.title: Mix'n'match
tools.description: GLAM catalog/Wikidata matching tool; red link lists on steroids.

tools.name: toolforge-weapon-of-mass-description
tools.title: Weapon of Mass Description
tools.description: This tool will allow users to describe (add labels) to Wikidata items en masse.

tools.name: mm_petscan
tools.title: PetScan
tools.description: PetScan can generate lists of Wikipedia (and related projects) pages or Wikidata items that match certain criteria, such as all pages in a certain category, or all items with a certain property. PetScan can also combine some temporary lists (here called 'sources') in various ways, to create a new one.

tools.name: mm_duplicity
tools.title: Duplicity
tools.description: Find Wikipedia articles without associated Wikidata items, and match or create a new item.

tools.name: toolforge-lexeme-forms
tools.title: Wikidata Lexeme Forms
tools.description: A tool to create Wikidata lexemes with a set of forms (e. g. declensions).
